channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Riots erupt over Australian Aboriginal girl’s murder as suspect arrested
item.description: Australian authorities appeal for calm after riots over killing of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Falklands claim: Can Argentina’s Milei use Trump ties to challenge the UK?
item.description: As US-UK relations nosedive, questions are mounting over whether Trump might shift Washington's position on the islands.

item.title: Who profits big from the war on Iran?
item.description: Who profits big from the war on Iran?

item.title: Oil prices rise again with little sign of war on Iran ending
item.description: Brent crude futures hit $126.41 on Thursday - a 5 percent rise for the week.

item.title: Despite growth and pay rises, Greek workers are among the poorest in Europe
item.description: Inflation has robbed them of a third of their income since the post-2009 global financial crisis, statistics show.

item.title: May Day in the age of AI: The new war on workers
item.description: As companies pour billions into AI, the fight for workers’ rights is being rewritten as a fight for the right to work.

item.title: Mohamed Salah ‘deserves big send-off’, says Liverpool boss Slot
item.description: Egypt international Mohamed Salah set to return for Liverpool before the season ends as Anfield exit looms.

item.title: More than 40 Iranian seafarers killed during US-Israeli war: Union leader
item.description: The Iranian Merchant Mariners Syndicate blamed US and Israeli attacks on Iran's ports and commercial fleet for deaths.

item.title: Rallies under way as workers gather for International Labour Day
item.description: Labour Day marches are being held as Iran war and rising energy prices threaten the global economy.

item.title: Commercial flights from Tehran’s main airport resume amid cautious normalcy
item.description: Flights are gradually back in the air, but uncertainty on the ground still lingers.

item.title: Ukraine begins to flex muscle as an emerging air power, angering Russia
item.description: Russian officials cast Ukrainian attacks as 'terrorism' as they urge 'vigilance'.

item.title: UK police charge man over stabbing of two Jewish men in London
item.description: British officials have raised the national terrorism threat to its second-highest level.

item.title: Iran vows ‘long, painful’ response if US renews attacks
item.description: Diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict have hit an impasse, with a ceasefire in place since April 8.

item.title: Russia hammers targets across Ukraine overnight
item.description: Attacks on towns on the Black Sea and in central Ukraine kill one, injure several.

item.title: What do Ukraine’s robot soldiers mean for the future of warfare?
item.description: Remote-controlled weapons have been used for some time, but AI is now on the cusp of making battlefield decisions.

item.title: On May Day, Gaza’s workers find whatever source of income they can
item.description: Gaza's economic collapse leaves workers in perilous jobs, with unemployment soaring amid ongoing devastation.

item.title: Why is piracy rising off Somalia again — and is the Iran war responsible?
item.description: At least four vessels have been hijacked in the past week or two, raising fears of a piracy resurgence.

item.title: Timberwolves eliminate Nuggets as Knicks demolish Hawks in NBA playoffs
item.description: Meanwhile, the Celtics-76ers playoff heads for a decisive Game 7 after Philadelphia beat Boston 106-93.

item.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 63 as Trump signals possible attacks
item.description: Iran’s president calls the US siege 'intolerable' as Donald Trump says war may resume.

item.title: Trump backs FIFA letting Iran play at World Cup in US
item.description: After months of campaigns and questions, FIFA says Iran will compete in the World Cup and matches will remain in the US.

item.title: New Banksy sculpture appears to show politician blinded by his own flag
item.description: Banksy has unveiled a new sculpture of a man stepping off a stone base with his face obscured by a flag.

item.title: Condemnation and protests against Israel’s raid of Gaza aid flotilla
item.description: Israel has provoked international condemnation for its interception of Gaza-bound aid boats in international waters.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US ports siege ‘intolerable’; Trump mulls action
item.description: Iran’s president says US siege of Iranian ports amounts to an “extension of military operations” by Washington.

item.title: Arsenal vs Fulham: Premier League – teams, start, lineups, title race
item.description: Arsenal will move six points clear of EPL title rivals Man City, who will have two games in hand, if they beat Fulham.

item.title: Palestine FA chief hits out at Israel federation VP at FIFA Congress
item.description: Palestine and Israel representatives had been lined up close together at the FIFA Congress in Canada.
